data.description:
## Why
Two defect classes are about the INVISIBLE AFFECTED SET (existing sites a plan silently breaks). A lexical trigger over-fires on descriptive prose; a code-grounded router is more reliable, and the heavy enumeration deserves its own context window.

## What (reusable mechanism, instantiated twice)
- Mechanism: G1/G2 (code-grounded) detects the pattern and EMITS AN OVERLAY TRIGGER; a dedicated agentic overlay then runs in its OWN context window, solely responsible for enumerating existing affected sites and verifying the plan handles each (migrate/exempt/gate).
- G-5: pattern = "pre-existing code may trigger a NEW prohibition" (block/reject/require-before/enforce/must-pass). Overlay greps the outlawed behaviours call sites, classifies migrated/exempted/uncovered.
- G-10: pattern = plan introduces a new git ref pattern / event source / schedule. Overlay enumerates every workflow trigger filter (INCLUDED/EXCLUDED/NO_FILTER) + release-infra coverage. Abstain-with-coverage on unparseable CI (polyglot fail-open).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reusable router->overlay mechanism (G1/G2 emits trigger; overlay runs in isolated context)
- [ ] G-5 prohibition instantiation with migrated/exempted/uncovered classification
- [ ] G-10 CI-trigger/release-infra instantiation, fail-open on unknown CI
- [ ] G4 consumer-enumeration precursor reuses the same worklist idiom
data.title: WS3: Enumeration-overlay pattern (code-grounded router -> dedicated overlay)
